fields.topic: Open Source Governance in the Global South: Challenges, Opportunities & Compliance in Africa
fields.description:
Open Source Governance in the Global South: Challenges, Opportunities & Compliance in Africa

In this talk, I share a practical and experience-driven view of how open-source projects and communities are governed in the Global South, with a strong focus on Africa and the Ghanaian developer ecosystem.

The session breaks down what open-source governance really means beyond code – including leadership, decision-making, contribution processes, and community sustainability. I also explore the key challenges facing African open-source communities such as informal structures, limited awareness of licensing and legal responsibilities, representation in global projects, and long-term sustainability.

Using real examples from developer and student communities in Ghana and across Africa, the talk highlights how strong governance and responsible open-source compliance (licensing, contributor management, community rules and regulatory awareness) can help African projects build trust, attract institutional adoption, and grow sustainably.

This session is aimed at developers, community leaders, organisations and open-source contributors who want to move African open-source projects from small community initiatives into trusted, scalable and globally relevant solutions.

fields.short_bio: SWE | DjangoCampus Founder| SpeakWise Co-Founder | Django | Ex GDSC Lead | DjangoCon Organizer | Flutter | FFSA | Google Crowdsource influencer | Community Manager | Open Source | AI |
fields.long_bio:
I’m a proud Individual Member of the Django Software Foundation and a full-stack software engineer with a strong focus on Django and mobile development. Beyond code, I’m deeply involved in the global Python, Django, Google, and FlutterFlow communities, actively contributing to the organization of several major conferences around the world.

I’ve served as the Opportunity Grant Co-Chair for DjangoCon US in both 2024 and 2025, and I was the Design Lead for PyCon Africa 2024 in Ghana, where I crafted the visual identity and led the creative direction for the entire conference. I'm also part of the organizing teams for DjangoCon Africa 2025 in Arusha, Tanzania, DjangoCon Europe, PyCon Ghana, and PyCon Portugal.

I’m the Founder of Django Campus, an initiative dedicated to empowering students and young developers to kick-start their tech careers through mentorship, practical training, and community engagement. Through Django Campus, we’ve helped many aspiring technologists across Ghana and other parts of Africa build skills, find confidence, and grow into impactful contributors in the tech ecosystem.

In addition to my community work, I’ve spoken at several local, international, and online conferences, including DjangoCon Africa, PyCon Africa, PyCon Namibia, and CHAOSS Africa, where I share insights on open source, community building, and software development.

Beyond the Python ecosystem, I also serve in ambassadorial and leadership roles with FlutterFlow and Google Developer Communities, fostering inclusive, empowering, and become good. I love coding as well

fields.short_bio: Software Engineer | Open Source Engineer | Security
fields.long_bio: I work on backend systems and security - the kind of stuff that breaks quietly if nobody pays attention. I co-founded OSSAfrica because open source shouldn't be a spectator sport on this continent, and I lead a SIG at OpenSSF to make sure the software and infrastructure people depend on is actually secure.

fields.short_bio: Software Engineer | Open source Advocate | Anything in-between
fields.long_bio:
I am a passionate software engineer specializing in backend development with Python, with experience in building scalable applications and contributing to open-source projects.

I actively contribute to open source through community engagements with the Python and PostgreSQL communities, volunteering, mentoring, and speaking at industry events.
I currently serve as the Python Wa Lead under Python Ghana and previously led Pyclub AAMUSTED.
 
I also volunteer as an organizer for DjangoCon US, a Diversity Committee Member at PostgreSQL US, a social media manager for PG DATA 2026, and a speaker at Local Python Events. 

My commitment to community involvement and continuous learning drives me to collaborate, share knowledge, and make meaningful contributions to the developer ecosystem.
